notes: Expanded stage-b/stage-c negative cases for ESRS to GS1 mapping. These cases should remain explicit no-mapping outcomes until ISA has evidence-backed structured sources that justify otherwise.

cases.rationale: Current GS1 product and trading-partner attributes do not directly encode employee living wage evidence or workforce remuneration policy disclosures.
Collective bargaining coverage is a labor-relations disclosure and is not represented as a standard GS1 product, logistics, or party master-data attribute.
Anti-corruption training completion is a governance-process disclosure and not a direct GS1 attribute in current sector models or WebVoc product vocabularies.
Community remediation outcomes and grievance resolution are stakeholder-impact records, not evidence-backed GS1 master-data attributes in the current ISA mapping corpus.
Consumer remediation resolution and complaint handling outcomes are service or governance records, not currently evidence-backed GS1 core attributes.
Whistleblowing case volumes and follow-up actions are compliance-governance records and are not represented as product or master-data fields in current GS1 sources.
Supplier workforce collective bargaining coverage is not a structured GS1 attribute in the current ISA source set and should remain an explicit no-mapping case.
Species recovery planning and biodiversity remediation governance are broader environmental management disclosures and not direct GS1 master-data attributes today.
